title: Public-records request workflow helper (compliance-positive only)
context: Officials often receive public-records requests (FOIA, sunshine law) and need guidance on how to respond lawfully and promptly. A workflow helper that walks them through the response process, response window, and applicable exemptions (correctly, with primary-source citations) is a valuable public-service feature. This helper must be strictly compliance-positive: it helps fulfill requests, never evade, delay, or minimize them. Evasion requests are refused and redirected to the lawful process. Expert sign-off required before shipping.
objective: Implement a public-records request workflow helper (mounted behind the guardrail layer) that guides an official through responding lawfully and promptly to a records request -- citing response windows, applicable exemptions, and the correct process -- while refusing any evasion, delay, or minimization framing.
acceptanceCriteria: Guides the official through the records-request response workflow: acknowledgment, response window, applicable exemptions (with citations), and the correct process for withholding (if any)
All cited exemptions and response rules sourced to primary law from the approved source dataset
Refuses and redirects any request to evade, delay, or minimize a valid records request; refusal includes a cited lawful-process pointer
Mounted behind the guardrail layer; evasion requests never reach the workflow helper
Labeled informational not legal advice; routes to the clerk/counsel for binding determinations
output: A merged PR implementing the public-records request workflow helper, compliance-positive and refusal-of-evasion enforced, behind the guardrail layer, with primary-source citations and expert sign-off.
